items.name: Combat Boots (Broken In)
items.description: Two pairs of proven, well-broken-in combat boots
items.whyNeeded: Ruck marches and land navigation demand boots that you fully trust. Blisters are disqualifying.
items.selectionTips: Break both pairs in over 6+ months of hard use. Rotate between evolutions to dry them out.

recommendedProducts.name: Danner Reckoning 8" Boot
recommendedProducts.reason: Worn by Marine Raiders and Army SF alike. Full-grain leather outlasts synthetic alternatives across Camp Lejeune's varied terrain through weeks of sustained use.

items.name: Rucksack (65L+ with Frame)
items.whyNeeded: MARSOC A&S includes heavy ruck marches over varied terrain at Camp Lejeune.
items.selectionTips: Frame is critical for weight distribution over long distances. Hip belt fit matters most.

recommendedProducts.name: ALICE Large Field Pack with Frame
recommendedProducts.reason: The original military standard for a reason. External aluminum frame is mandatory for MARSOC ruck weights and distributes load far better than frameless packs.

items.name: Lensatic Compass
items.whyNeeded: Land navigation is a primary MARSOC evaluation skill.
items.selectionTips: Practice daily for months. Know how to navigate at night.

recommendedProducts.name: Cammenga 3H Tritium Lensatic Compass
recommendedProducts.reason: Identical to the MARSOC-issued compass. Accurate to 1° in any lighting condition — non-negotiable for selection-level land navigation in the dark.

items.name: Hydration System
items.whyNeeded: North Carolina heat and humidity make hydration survival-critical during ruck marches.
items.selectionTips: CamelBak or similar. Keep it filled and drink before you're thirsty.

recommendedProducts.name: CamelBak BFM 3L Hydration Pack
recommendedProducts.reason: Hands-free 3L hydration with a military-grade bladder. The extra molle pouches stow essential field items without requiring a second separate pack.

items.name: Wool Socks (Quality)
items.description: Merino wool or military boot socks
items.whyNeeded: Moisture management is critical for blister prevention.
items.selectionTips: Darn Tough only. Bring 10+ pairs and change every evolution.

recommendedProducts.name: Darn Tough Boot Full Cushion Sock
recommendedProducts.reason: Rated for the harshest field conditions. Wool keeps feet regulated through Camp Lejeune's unpredictable heat, humidity, and water crossings.

items.name: Headlamp (Red Filter)
items.whyNeeded: Night land navigation requires a reliable light source that preserves night vision.
items.selectionTips: Bring extra AA batteries. Test it before reporting.

recommendedProducts.name: Princeton Tec Remix Pro Headlamp
recommendedProducts.reason: Trusted by Marine Raiders for mission reliability. Multiple brightness modes and months of battery life on standard AAs — no charging cable needed in the field.

items.name: Leukotape
items.selectionTips: Apply preemptively to blister-prone areas. Non-negotiable at this level.

items.name: Electrolyte Supplements
items.selectionTips: Camp Lejeune heat is intense. Cramping ends selection careers.

recommendedProducts.name: Liquid IV Hydration Multiplier
recommendedProducts.reason: Clinically proven 2–3× faster hydration than water alone. Camp Lejeune summer heat causes dangerous cramping without proper electrolyte supplementation.

items.name: GPS Devices
items.notes: All navigation is map and compass only. GPS found on your person = dropped from selection.

items.name: Pre-Workout Stimulants
items.notes: Risk of cardiac events under extreme physical stress.
